article_title: Lilly's VERVE-102 Base Editor Cuts LDL Cholesterol by 62% in Phase 1b Trial, Results Published in NEJM
summary: Submission approvable with 2 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Lilly's VERVE-102 Base Editor Cuts LDL Cholesterol by 62% in Phase 1b Trial, Results Published in NEJM
content_preview.summary: A single infusion of VERVE-102 reduced LDL-C by up to 62% and PCSK9 by up to 88% in a 35-patient trial, with effects sustained up to 18 months.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Eli Lilly on May 25, 2026, announced Phase 1b results for VERVE-102, an investigational gene-editing therapy that silences a cholesterol-regulating gene in the liver with a single infusion. At the highest dose tested, the drug reduced levels of the PCSK9 protein by up to 88% and low-density lipoprotein cholesterol (LDL-C) by up to 62%, with effects held stable for up to 18 months, according to the [Lilly press release](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/a-single-dose-of-lillys...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article with appropriate technical depth for a biomedical gene-editing trial. The What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis structure is well-executed. Word count (1060) is within the 400–1200 News range. The article correctly contextualizes Phase 1b limitations and does not overstate the findings.
editor_notes.source_verification: 7 of 8 sources verified from disk snapshots (source-0 through source-6). Source-7 (NEJM, https://www.nejm.org/doi/full/10.1056/NEJMoa2601283) returned HTTP 403 — no snapshot available; the publication claim is corroborated by all other sources. Source-0 (Lilly PR Newswire, source-0.html.gz): confirms headline stats (62% LDL-C, 88% PCSK9, 35 patients, 18-month durability, six dose cohorts, no treatment-related SAEs). Source-1 (BioSpace, source-1.html.gz): mirrors the PR; confirms Kathiresan quote verbatim and trial design details. Source-2 (BioPharma Dive, source-2.html.gz): confirms Phase 2 initiation by end of 2026 and the $1B Verve acquisition context. Source-3 (STAT News, source-3.html.gz): paywalled beyond lede; accessible text confirms VERVE-101 safety concern context and 'world's leading killer' attribution. Source-4 (STAT News pharmalot, source-4.html.gz): paywalled beyond lede; confirms 62% LDL reduction framing. Source-5 (Lilly acquisition PR, source-5.html.gz): confirms acquisition price ($10.50/share, ~$1.0B upfront, up to $3.00/share CVR, total ~$1.3B), date (June 17, 2025), Ruth Gimeno quote — but title discrepancy found (see factual_accuracy). Source-6 (Investing News Network, source-6.html.gz): syndication of Lilly press release; confirms all headline figures and durability claim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: One title misattribution found: the article states 'Ruth Gimeno, Lilly's group president for cardiometabolic health' but source-5 (Lilly acquisition press release, source-5.html.gz) identifies her as 'Ruth Gimeno, Lilly group vice president, Diabetes and Metabolic Research and Development.' This is a recoverable correction — the quote itself is accurate and the substance is unaffected. Additionally, the article states results were presented at 'the 94th European Atherosclerosis Society (EAS) Congress in Athens, Greece' — the EAS Congress and simultaneous NEJM publication are confirmed by multiple sources, but the ordinal '94th' and location 'Athens, Greece' do not appear in any of the 7 available snapshots. These are subordinate descriptive details in the Overview section (not the headline or summary) and are plausible for the 2026 EAS Congress, but they are unverifiable from captured snapshots. All other quantitative claims (62% LDL-C, 88% PCSK9, 35 patients, six dose cohorts, 9-month median follow-up, 15 patients at ≥1 year, 18-month durability, no treatment-related SAEs, FDA Fast Track designation, $1.0B acquisition price, $10.50/share, CVR up to $3.00/share) trace to cited sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism, no editorializing. The Analysis section appropriately contextualizes Phase 1b limitations (small N, interim data, cardiovascular outcomes vs surrogate endpoints) without dismissing the results.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate coverage found. A related article on Scribe Therapeutics STX-1150 (published 2026-05-26) covers a different PCSK9-targeting gene therapy by a different company with a different mechanism at a different stage (first-in-human clearance vs Phase 1b results). The Lilly/Verve VERVE-102 Phase 1b NEJM publication is a distinct, original news event.
concerns: Ruth Gimeno's title is misattributed: article says 'group president for cardiometabolic health'; source says 'group vice president, Diabetes and Metabolic Research and Development'
The '94th' EAS Congress ordinal and 'Athens, Greece' venue are unverified from available source snapshots; these appear in the Overview but not in any of the 7 captured sources
investingnews.com is not in the source allowlist, though the snapshot confirms it is a faithful syndication of the Lilly press release with no editorial additions
recommendations: File a corrections record noting the Ruth Gimeno title misattribution
Consider adding investingnews.com to config/source_allowlist.txt as it functions as a press release syndication outlet
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively strong submission. All core facts (efficacy figures, trial design, safety profile, acquisition price, regulatory status) are confirmed by cited sources. The article is APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS: the Ruth Gimeno title error is a single correctable specific that does not affect the article's lead or headline, and the unverified EAS Congress specifics are subordinate description that can be noted. The article is otherwise publication-ready.
